cards.back: An in-place comparison-based sorting algorithm that selects the smallest element from the unsorted portion of the list and swaps it with the first unsorted element.
Bubble Sort is an in-place comparison-based sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort?
cards.back: The time complexity of Selection Sort is O(n^2), making it less efficient for large datasets compared to other sorting algorithms.

cards.front: What is the advantage of Bubble Sort?
cards.back: Bubble Sort is a stable sorting algorithm meaning that equal elements remain in their original positions after sorting, and it has a simple implementation.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
cards.back: The time complexity of Bubble Sort is also O(n^2) in the worst-case scenario, although it can be improved to O(n) in the best-case scenario if the input list is already sorted.
